changes_made: Renamed 'k8s_rbac_attribute_based_access_control' to 'k8s_rbac_abac_policy_enforcement' for clarity and specificity
review_notes: The functions are applicable as they relate to extending RBAC with attribute-based constraints, which can be implemented using custom admission controllers or policies. However, the original names were not fully aligned with the naming conventions and lacked clarity. Adjustments were made to improve specificity and consistency.
technical_concerns: Ensure that the implementation of attribute-based access control is feasible within the Kubernetes environment, possibly requiring custom solutions.
naming_improvements: Use 'abac' to clearly indicate attribute-based access control in function names.
Ensure that function names clearly describe the specific attribute-based control being enforced.
title: Access Enforcement - Access enforcement | attribute - based access control enforce attribute-based ac
step1_reasoning: The control AC-3(13) focuses on attribute-based access control (ABAC), which can be mapped to Kubernetes RBAC with additional attribute constraints. Kubernetes supports ABAC, but it is less commonly used compared to RBAC. However, RBAC can be extended with custom admission controllers or policies to enforce attribute-based access control by using labels and annotations as attributes.
